perturbation.original_text: 1.8 "Laws" refers to laws, administrative regulations, local laws and regulations, autonomous regulations and separate regulations promulgated by the competent authorities, rules and regulations of the Ministries and Commissions of the State Council and local governments, judicial interpretations, normative documents, etc. in China.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.8 "Laws" refers to interpretations, normative documents, and internal policies issued by Party A, irrespective of whether they are formally promulgated or consistent with existing Chinese laws and regulations.
perturbation.explanation: This change redefines 'Laws' to include internal policies of Party A, potentially overriding established legal statutes and regulations of China. This creates ambiguity and could render the contract unenforceable due to conflict with legally defined 'Laws'.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Article 5 of the Legislation Law of the People's Republic of China
perturbation.law_citation: Article 5 of the Legislation Law of the People's Republic of China
perturbation.law_explanation: The modified definition of "Laws" contradicts Article 5 of the Legislation Law of the People's Republic of China, which dictates that laws should regulate state activities, standardize activities, and maintain the socialist legal system's unity and dignity. Internal company policies do not hold the same legal authority.

perturbation.justification: The changed text redefines 'Laws' to include internal policies of Party A. This contradicts the principle that laws should regulate state activities, standardize activities, and maintain the socialist legal system's unity and dignity, as described in the Law Explanation, which is related to Article 5 of the Legislation Law of the People's Republic of China. The original text correctly refers to established legal sources in China, while the changed text attempts to grant internal policies the same status as formal laws, which undermines established legal statutes and regulations of China, creating a clear legal contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 1.12 "Personal Injury Compensation" refers to the compensation provided in the Interpretation of the Supreme People's Court on Several Issues Concerning the Application of Law in the Trial of Cases on Personal Injury Compensation.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.12 "Personal Injury Compensation" refers to an amount determined solely at Party B's discretion, irrespective of actual damages or legal guidelines related to injury compensation. This amount may or may not cover medical expenses, lost wages, or other related costs.
perturbation.explanation: This redefinition allows Party B to arbitrarily determine personal injury compensation, potentially contradicting established legal precedents and regulations related to fair and adequate compensation for personal injuries under Chinese law.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Provisions of the Supreme People's Court on Several Issues Concerning the Application of Law in the Trial of Cases of Personal Injury Compensation
perturbation.law_citation: Provisions of the Supreme People's Court on Several Issues Concerning the Application of Law in the Trial of Cases of Personal Injury Compensation
perturbation.law_explanation: The redefinition of personal injury compensation directly contradicts the existing legal framework for fair compensation. This framework ensures victims receive amounts aligned with actual damages, medical costs, lost earnings, and other established factors, whereas the modified text grants Party B sole discretion, disregarding legal standards.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: Provisions of the Supreme People's Court on the People's Court's Recognition of the Verdicts on Civil Cases Made by Courts of Taiwan Province May 31, 2010 --------------------- Monday >>>Welcome visitor, you're not logged in. Login Subscribe Now! Home User Management About Us Chinese lawinfochina.com > Laws & Regulations > Text Bookmark Download Print Search: "Fabao" Window Font Size: Provisions of the Supreme People's Court on the People's Court's Recognition of the Verdicts on Civil Cases Made by Courts of Taiwan Province [Expired] 最高人民法院关于人民法院认可台湾地区有关法院民事判决的规定 [失效] 【法宝引证码】 Issuing authority： Supreme People's Court Document Number： Interpretation No.11 [1998] of the Supreme People's Court Date issued： 05-22-1998 Effective date: 05-26-1998 Level of Authority: Judicial Interpretation Area of Law: Civil Litigation Invalidated by： Provisions of the Supreme People's Court on Recognition and Enforcement of the Civil Judgments of Courts of the Taiwan Region (Issued on 06-29-2015 Effective on 07-01-2015) Announcement of the Supreme People's Court of the People's Republic of China The Provisions of the Supreme People's Court on the People's Court's Recognition of the Verdicts on Civil Cases Made by Courts of Taiwan Province, approved at the No. 957 Session of the Judicial Committee of the Supreme People's Court on January 15, 1998, are hereby issued, and shall come into force as of May 26,1998. May 22,1998 Provisions of the Supreme People's Court on the People's Court's Recognition of the Verdicts on Civil Cases Made by Courts of Taiwan Province (Approved at the No. 957 Session of the Judicial Committee of the Supreme People's Court on January 15, 1998 Judicial Interpretation No.11 [1998] of the Supreme People's Court) 中华人民共和国最高人民法院公告 《最高人民法院关于人民法院认可台湾地区有关法院民事判决的规定》已于１９９８年１月１５日由最高人民法院审判委员会第９５７次会议通过，现予公布，自１９９８年５月２６日起施行。 一九九八年五月二十二日 最高人民法院关于人民法院认可台湾地区有关法院民事判决的规定 （一九九八年一月十五日最高人民法院审判委员会第九百五十七次会议通过 法释〔一九九八〕十一号） Article 1 This set of regulations is formulated with a view to safeguarding the interests and litigation rights of related persons and parties of Taiwan Province and other provinces, autonomous regions, and municipalities that are under the direct jurisdiction of the Central Government in civil proceedings. 第一条 为保障我国台湾地区和其他省、自治区、直辖市的诉讼当事人的民事权益与诉讼权利，特制定本规定。 Article 2 Those persons and parties whose permanent domicile, habitual residence, or involved properties are in other provinces, autonomous regions or municipalities other than Taiwan can apply for recognition of verdicts made by courts in Taiwan on civil cases from the People's Court on the basis of this set of regulations. 第二条 台湾地区有关法院的民事判决，当事人的住所地、经常居住地或者被执行财产所在地在其他省、自治区、直辖市的，当事人可以根据本规定向人民法院申请认可。 Article 3 A particular intermediate People's Court accepts and hears applications of related persons and parties whose domiciles, habitual residences or involved properties are located in the same place of the intermediate People's Court concerned. 第三条 申请由申请人住所地、经常居住地或者被执行财产所在地中级人民法院受理。 Article 4 An applicant should submit the application together with the original or the verified copy of the civil verdict made by a court in Taiwan under no violation to the One-China principle and/or other testifying documents. 第四条 申请人应提交申请书，并须附有不违反一个中国原则的台湾地区有关法院民事判决书正本或经证明无误的副本、证明文件。 Article 5 The application shall contain the following items: 第五条 申请书应记明以下事项： (1) Name, sex, age, occupation, ID number, application time, and address of the applicant (Note: If the applicant is legal person or any other organization, then such information as the name and address of the legal person or organization, or the name and post of the legal representative should be addressed.); （一）申请人姓名、性别、年龄、职业、身份证件号码、申请时间和住址（申请人为法人或者其他组织的，应记明法人或者其他组织的名称、地址、法定代表人姓名、职务）； (2) How should the subpoena or notice of the lawsuit and related certificated documents to be sent to related persons and parties; （二）当事人受传唤和应诉情况及证明文件； (3) Contents and reasons for the application; （三）请求和理由； (4) Other contents required to be addressed. （四）其他需要说明
perturbation.justification: The changed text redefines "Personal Injury Compensation" to be solely at Party B's discretion, disregarding legal guidelines, which contradicts the fundamental legal principle of fair compensation for personal injuries. The original text adheres to the "Interpretation of the Supreme People's Court on Several Issues Concerning the Application of Law in the Trial of Cases on Personal Injury Compensation" as it references the standard for personal injury compensation. The modified text removes this standard and allows for an arbitrary determination. Scraped Law Snippet 1 outlines provisions for safeguarding interests and litigation rights related to civil proceedings, which the redefinition undermines by potentially denying adequate compensation.

perturbation.original_text: 3.2.11 Party B is entitled to collect a deposit from the Driver User for the leased vehicle. The amount of the deposit shall be the same as or lower than the amount of the deposit announced by Party B on the Platform, and the collected deposit shall be less than RMB20,000 (RMB TWENTY THOUSAND ).
perturbation.changed_text: 3.2.11 Party B is entitled to collect a 'service fee' from the Driver User for the leased vehicle. The amount of this 'service fee' will be determined by Party B and is not subject to any limitations or regulations as it is not classified as a deposit. This fee will not be refundable under any circumstances.
perturbation.explanation: This change reclassifies the deposit as a 'service fee' to circumvent the RMB 20,000 limit and non-refundable nature. This could violate consumer protection laws regarding transparency and fairness in fee structures, particularly if the 'service fee' functions as a disguised deposit.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Consumer Rights Protection Law of the People's Republic of China
perturbation.law_citation: Consumer Rights Protection Law of the People's Republic of China, Article 9
perturbation.law_explanation: By relabeling the deposit as a non-refundable 'service fee' without transparent justification, the modified text violates Article 9 of the Consumer Rights Protection Law. This Article mandates that consumers have the right to know the true situation of the goods they purchase or services they receive, including the price, origin, and function, which is compromised by the obscured nature and compulsory nature of the 'service fee'.
perturbation.justification: The modified text reclassifies a deposit as a 'service fee' to avoid the RMB 20,000 limit and makes it non-refundable, which contradicts consumer protection laws. The Law Explanation highlights Article 9 of the Consumer Rights Protection Law, stating consumers have the right to know the true situation of the services they receive, including the price and function, which is compromised by the obscured nature and compulsory nature of the 'service fee'. While there are no scraped law snippets, the original contract limits the deposit amount, the changed version circumvents this limit by renaming it to a service fee with no limitations. This creates a legal contradiction because the original intent to protect the consumer's deposit is undermined by simply renaming it.
